title: TA505 / Clop Detection & Hardening Plan
subtitle: An assume-breach detection and hardening plan for a flat, single-domain Windows estate (~1,600 servers, ~7,300 workstations) with no central SIEM. Defends against the documented 2019 TA505/Clop chain and scores coverage against the paired adversary-emulation plan.
scope: Flat single-domain Windows estate; MDR + native consoles, no central SIEM

executive_summary.threat_one_liner: TA505 (Grace / Gold Tahoe) runs a patient, dual-use big-game intrusion: phished macro-Excel foothold, ~15-minute C2 beacon, EternalBlue lateral movement, AD recon to Domain Admin, Cobalt Strike on the domain controllers, fleet-wide AV disable, then Clop encryption of servers and reachable backups.
executive_summary.coverage_one_liner: Of the 8 pre-ransomware techniques in scope, 3 are natively detected by the endpoint MDR, 3 are covered only by assume-breach hunts against thin telemetry, and 2 remain gaps because the log source is not collected or not retained.

top_gaps.gap: No central log collection: DNS, proxy, and DC auth telemetry live on islands with short retention, so beacon periodicity and AD recon cannot be detected or hunted reliably.
top_gaps.impact: The entire ~5-week Domain-Admin dwell window passes unseen.

top_gaps.gap: Domain controllers and many Tier-0 servers have no EDR agent, so Cobalt Strike beaconing on the control plane generates no watched signal.
top_gaps.impact: The attacker's control layer on Tier-0 is invisible.

top_gaps.gap: Firewall / DNS retention is 14 days, shorter than the beacon-hunt window, so a periodicity hunt cannot look back far enough.
top_gaps.impact: Slow, low-and-slow C2 defeats the only available hunt.

log_sources.name: Endpoint MDR (workstations)
log_sources.notes: Behavioural alerts triaged by the MDR SOC; good workstation coverage.

log_sources.name: Endpoint EDR (servers / DCs)
log_sources.notes: No agent on domain controllers and much of the Tier-0 server plane; the control layer is blind.

log_sources.name: Mail gateway (link / attachment)
log_sources.notes: Detonation verdicts logged; a user phishing report exists but triage is ad hoc.

log_sources.name: Web proxy / DNS
log_sources.notes: Per-faculty resolvers; no aggregation; 14-day retention too short for a beacon-periodicity hunt.

log_sources.name: Firewall (perimeter)
log_sources.notes: Flow logs retained 14 days; not correlated with endpoint or DNS.

log_sources.name: Domain controller security log
log_sources.notes: Default local retention only; LDAP query logging not enabled, so AD recon is invisible.

log_sources.name: Sysmon (Tier-0 pilot)
log_sources.notes: EID 10 (LSASS access) captured on a pilot subset of servers; not forwarded anywhere.

log_sources.name: File-server audit / backup console
log_sources.notes: Object-access auditing partial; ransomware canary not configured; backups reachable from the production domain.

techniques.comment: Mail gateway link detonation + MDR on click
DNS/firewall island logs, 14-day retention, no beacon analytic
MS17-010 patched + SMBv1 disabled; residual on legacy hosts
Assume-breach LDAP-recon hunt; no native detection
Sysmon EID 10 hunt on Tier-0 pilot; partial coverage
File-server audit exists; canary not built; fires at impact only

detections.name: Office application spawns script/shell host
detections.logic_prose: Alert when winword.exe or excel.exe spawns a child in {wscript.exe, cscript.exe, powershell.exe, cmd.exe, mshta.exe, regsvr32.exe}, especially with an outbound network connection in the same process tree.
false_positives: Legitimate finance macros that shell out to a reporting tool
detections.tuning_note: Allow-list the two known finance macro signers by certificate thumbprint; keep the network-connection condition to cut noise.

triage.first_action: Isolate the host via MDR and pull the full process tree

detections.name: Mass endpoint-protection disable across many hosts
detections.logic_prose: Correlate AV/EDR service-stop or tamper events (e.g. Windows Event 7040/1116, MDR tamper alerts) across hosts; fire when N>5 distinct hosts disable protection within a 30-minute window.
false_positives: Coordinated agent upgrade or maintenance window
detections.tuning_note: Suppress during approved change windows only; a fleet-wide disable outside a change is the loudest pre-ransomware signal there is.

triage.first_action: Assume imminent detonation: begin containment and verify backup isolation

detections.name: Phishing link detonation verdict on delivered mail
detections.logic_prose: Alert when the gateway returns a malicious/suspicious verdict for a URL or attachment that was already delivered to a mailbox, or when a user submits a phishing report matching a delivered message.
false_positives: Benign newsletters flagged by URL reputation
detections.tuning_note: Auto-open a ticket and route to the service desk queue; the missed 2019 user report is the reason this must page a human.

triage.first_action: Claw back the message and check proxy/DNS for click-through

detections.name: C2 beacon periodicity (regular-interval callout)
detections.logic_prose: Flag a source host that contacts the same destination at a near-constant interval (~15 min, low jitter) over many hours to a newly-registered or low-reputation domain.
false_positives: Software update pollers and telemetry SDKs
detections.tuning_note: Cannot run today: proxy/DNS are island logs with 14-day retention and no aggregation. This is a gap, not a live rule.

triage.first_action: Block the destination and hunt the source host

detections.name: LSASS credential-access (memory read)
detections.logic_prose: Alert on Sysmon EID 10 where TargetImage is lsass.exe and GrantedAccess includes 0x1010/0x1410 from a non-allow-listed process.
false_positives: EDR/AV and legitimate LSASS-reading admin tools
detections.tuning_note: Only the Tier-0 Sysmon pilot is instrumented and nothing forwards the events, so this runs as a hunt today, not a native alert.

triage.first_action: Force-reset exposed credentials; treat the host as compromised

hunts.hypothesis: An attacker has run AD reconnaissance (PingCastle / AdFind / PowerSploit) against the domain to map privileged accounts and the path to Domain Admin.
hunts.rationale: There is no native LDAP-recon detection and DC query logging is off; recon is the quiet step before escalation, so it must be hunted.
hunts.analytic_logic: Look for a single host issuing a burst of directory-enumeration queries, or tools writing AdFind/PingCastle output files; baseline against known admin workstations.
hunts.outcome_detail: No recon bursts in the 30-day pilot window, but DC logs only retain 7 days locally so the look-back is shallow.
hunts.what_negative_proves: A negative only clears the last 7 days of DC-local logs; it does NOT clear the full dwell window and must not be read as 'no recon happened'.
hunts.next_action: Enable DC LDAP query logging and forward 4662 events so this hunt can look back further than local retention.

hunts.hypothesis: A host beacons to external C2 at a regular ~15-minute interval, hidden in normal web traffic.
hunts.rationale: The signature C2 signal (SDBBot ~15-min beacon) has no live detection because proxy/DNS are not aggregated; a baseline of per-host external-destination periodicity would surface it.
hunts.scope: Internet-egress from the workstation and server VLANs, trailing 14 days
hunts.analytic_logic: Group connections by (src, dst) and compute inter-arrival regularity; rank low-jitter, long-lived pairs to rare/new domains at the top.
hunts.outcome_detail: 14-day retention is shorter than a low-and-slow beacon window and the island logs cannot be joined, so the baseline is unreliable.
hunts.what_negative_proves: Nothing durable: with 14-day island retention a clean result is 'we could not look', not 'no beacon'.
hunts.next_action: Stand up central log collection with 90-day egress retention, then re-run monthly.

hunts.hypothesis: Credential-dumping or defense-evasion activity occurred on Tier-0 before any alert existed.
hunts.rationale: EDR is absent on DCs and only a Sysmon pilot covers some servers, so a retrospective sweep of the pilot data may find LSASS access or service-stop clusters missed live.
hunts.scope: Tier-0 Sysmon pilot subset, trailing 14 days
hunts.analytic_logic: Score processes reading lsass.exe by rarity of the parent image and command line; cluster AV service-stop events by host and time.
hunts.outcome_detail: One benign match: an approved EDR-migration script read LSASS during a maintenance window; confirmed with the platform team and allow-listed.
hunts.what_negative_proves: A negative covers only the pilot hosts; the un-instrumented DCs and most servers remain unhunted, so absence of evidence is not coverage.
hunts.next_action: Expand Sysmon to all Tier-0 and forward events; deploy EDR to domain controllers.

backlog.control: Central log collection + MDR extension (EDR on servers/DCs, forward DNS/DC/proxy)
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Turns the ~5-week silent dwell (beacon, recon, C2 on DCs) from invisible into detectable/huntable.
backlog.residual_gap: Detection quality still depends on rule tuning and analyst review cadence.
backlog.sme_note: Extend the existing vendor MDR rather than build a SIEM; IG2 stretch but the single highest-leverage move.

backlog.control: MFA on all privileged and remote access; remove standing Domain Admin
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Makes dumped credentials far less usable and blunts the leap from foothold to Domain Admin.
backlog.residual_gap: Pass-the-hash within a session still possible until tiering lands.
backlog.sme_note: IG1 floor; mostly configuration on existing identity tooling.

backlog.control: Patch SLA incl. known-exploited (KEV); emergency-patch MS17-010, disable SMBv1
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Removes the EternalBlue lateral-movement path (red AEP step 4).
backlog.residual_gap: Legacy appliances that cannot be patched need compensating isolation.
backlog.sme_note: IG1; SMBv1 disable is free and immediate on modern Windows.

backlog.control: AD tiering and network segmentation (isolate Tier-0, server, and backup planes)
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Breaks unimpeded lateral movement to 267 servers and stops one foothold reaching everything.
backlog.residual_gap: Full tiering is a multi-quarter programme; sequence Tier-0 isolation first.
backlog.sme_note: IG2 stretch; start with a Tier-0 admin boundary and backup-plane isolation.

backlog.control: Offline / immutable backups verified by restore test
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Preserves a recovery path even if encryption succeeds (2019: online backups were reachable and lost).
backlog.residual_gap: Immutability window must exceed attacker dwell; test restores quarterly.
backlog.sme_note: IG1; the single control that most changes the outcome of a successful detonation.

backlog.control: Block internet macros; tamper-protect endpoint AV/EDR
backlog.attacker_step_closed: Closes the macro front door and stops the one-click fleet-wide AV disable.
backlog.residual_gap: Signed internal macros still need an allow-list and review.
backlog.sme_note: IG1; both are policy toggles on existing Microsoft 365 and MDR tooling.

now: Block macros from the internet and turn on EDR tamper protection (rank 6) - policy toggles, same day.
Enforce MFA on privileged and remote access (rank 2).
Isolate and verify offline/immutable backups with a restore test (rank 5).
later: Extend MDR with central log collection and EDR on servers/DCs (rank 1) - the big-bet detection uplift.
Deliver AD tiering and network segmentation, Tier-0 first (rank 4).

steps.controlOrDetectionRef: Beacon analytic - no aggregated egress logs
steps.ownerOrNextStep: Network team: 90-day central egress retention (backlog rank 1)
Platform team: expand Sysmon + EDR to DCs

seats.name: Detection engineer
seats.stance: Detect what the MDR can see natively; be honest that egress and DC telemetry are gaps, not rules
seats.summary: The workstation MDR gives strong coverage of the macro-execution and mass-AV-disable steps. The loud pre-ransomware signals (beaconing, AD recon, C2 on DCs) are unwatched or uncollected, so they are written as hunts and backlog items, not as detections we can claim.

seats.name: Threat hunter
seats.stance: Where telemetry does not cover the window, treat it as suspect, not clear
seats.summary: Three hunts cover the recon, beacon, and credential-access steps that have no native detection. All three are retention-limited today: a clean hunt over 7-14 days of island logs is 'we could not look back far enough', not 'nothing happened'. Central collection is the precondition for trustworthy hunting.

seats.name: Hardening architect
seats.stance: IG1 floor first; sequence quick wins before the big bets
seats.summary: Four IG1 quick wins (macros, patch/SMBv1, MFA, offline backups) can land now and defeat the highest-leverage steps in the chain. The two big bets (central logging/MDR extension and AD tiering) are IG2 stretch and carry the detection and blast-radius uplift; they are sequenced later but are the durable fixes.

verified: The ATT&CK technique IDs and the attacker chain are taken from the paired red-team AEP and the publicly documented 2019 incident.
The endpoint MDR provides 24/7 triage for workstation behavioural alerts (macro-spawn, mass AV disable).
Domain controllers currently have no EDR agent and DC LDAP query logging is disabled.
unverified: Exact proxy/DNS/firewall retention (assumed 14 days) - confirm per faculty before relying on the beacon hunt.
Which Tier-0 servers are covered by the Sysmon pilot - the LSASS hunt only covers the instrumented subset.
Whether the mail gateway retroactively re-scores already-delivered links - confirm with the vendor before trusting detection #3.
